turns.text: Thanks for calling Sears Home Services — what can I help you with today?
My laundry never seems to actually finish, it's driving me nuts.
I hear you — let's sort it out. Is the trouble with the washer not finishing its cycle, or the dryer not getting clothes dry?
The clothes are still damp after a full cycle — it's the dryer, a Maytag.
Got it — a Maytag dryer leaving clothes damp. When did it start, and does it show any error code or make an unusual sound?
It runs the whole time but stays cool, started about a week ago. No codes.

A dryer that runs but stays cool almost always comes down to airflow or the heat source. Clean the lint screen and check the exterior vent for a blockage first — a clogged vent traps heat and moisture. If the drum is spinning but never warms even with clear airflow, the heating element may be failing.
Okay that makes sense, I'll check the vent.
Perfect — if it's still coming out damp with a clear vent, call back and we'll get a technician to look at the heating element.

symptoms.description: clothes still damp after a full cycle, dryer runs but stays cool

steps_given: Check the exterior vent for a blockage.
If airflow is clear but the dryer never warms, plan to inspect the heating element.
